name: George Brunies
abstract: George Clarence Brunies (February 6, 1902 – November 19, 1974), a.k.a. Georg Brunis, was an American jazz trombonist, who was part of the dixieland revival.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others
Newport Jazz Festival: Eddie Condon, Lee Wiley, The World's Greatest Jazz Band, and Others

about_quotes.speaker: Chauncey Morehouse
about_quotes.evidence: Then we come down to your band, Chauncey Morehouse and his Swing Six. Charlie Spivak on trumpet, George Brunies -trombone... and you on the drums and vibes.

about_quotes.speaker: Frank Netto
about_quotes.evidence: In fact I played a couple of jobs with him on the bass fiddle, you know

about_quotes.speaker: Muggsy Spanier
about_quotes.evidence: George was with Ted Lewis about 16 years and I was with him about seven... all the records that you were on, George would be on then, too

about_quotes.speaker: Theodore Saunders
about_quotes.evidence: Sharky Bananas had his band. So we went down there and we played on the boat, and whats' name, George Brunis was with us, yeah.

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy McPartland
about_quotes.evidence: when I had Peewee Russell and George Brunis, Joe Sullivan, Eddie Condon and Dave Tough on drums. A good band.

about_quotes.speaker: Henry A. "Hank" Kmen
about_quotes.evidence: He had Bill Bourgeois on clarinet, and Johnny Castang on drums, and George Brunis on trombone... I used to go down there often and sit in with the band.
